source: CFPB Consumer Response Annual Report 2025, Section 4.1 and Section 5, Pages 18, 75

credit_repair_organizations.description: Credit repair orgs misusing the complaint process
credit_repair_organizations.source_quote: credit repair organizations and those who offer credit advice online

finfluencers.description: Social media influencers promoting complaint filing

ai_agents.description: Large language models and autonomous software flooding system
ai_agents.source_quote: large language models and autonomous software systems ('AI Agents') only further complicate this area and create greater demands on government and companies generally
ai_agents.impact: empower bad actors to flood the system with duplicative and spurious submissions

suspected_fraud_by_consumer.note: One nationwide CRA accounted for more than 90% of these

duplicate_complaint.note: One nationwide CRA accounted for more than 60% of these

unauthorized_third_party.note: One nationwide CRA accounted for more than 60% of these

planned_reforms: Align complaint process with FCRA statutory requirements
Direct consumers to first file disputes with CRAs before submitting to CFPB
Implement new authentication measures to root out malignant actors
Guard against those who use complaint system to circumvent FCRA dispute process
cfpb_response.source_quote: No longer will the Bureau have a complaint system that undermines the statutorily established credit dispute procedures under FCRA

key_insight: AI agents and credit repair orgs are flooding the system. One CRA alone flagged 469,800 complaints as suspected consumer fraud.
